options.B: ϕX(·) is periodic with period a

Thus, ϕX(t) is periodic with period a, proving statement (B) true.
Step 5: Analysis of other options.
(C) |ϕX(t)| < 1 for all t ̸= a is not necessarily true because for a discrete distribution, ϕX(t)
may attain 1 at other multiples of a.
(D) The integral expression does not hold in this general setting—it is unrelated to the
periodicity condition and characteristic function form.
Step 6: Conclusion.
Therefore, both statements (A) and (B) are correct.
Quick Tip
If a random variable takes discrete values that are integer multiples of a constant, its
characteristic function is periodic with a period equal to the reciprocal of that constant.
